title: Construction and destruction order with two objects
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write a `Base` class and a `Derived` class (publicly inheriting from `Base`) whose constructors and destructors print trace messages. Then create **two** `Derived` objects in the same scope so the trace reveals two rules at once: the base portion is built before the derived portion, and objects are destroyed in the reverse of the order they were created.

## Input

One line with two strings (no spaces): `firstName secondName`.

## Output

With the two `Derived` objects named `first` and `second` (created in that order, in one scope), the program must print:

```
Base constructed
Derived(<firstName>) constructed
Base constructed
Derived(<secondName>) constructed
Derived(<secondName>) destroyed
Base destroyed
Derived(<firstName>) destroyed
Base destroyed
```

## Constraints

- `Base` prints `Base constructed` when constructed and `Base destroyed` when destroyed.
- `Derived` stores the name it was given and prints `Derived(<name>) constructed` / `Derived(<name>) destroyed`.
- Do not hard-code the message order: it must fall out of constructing `first` then `second` in a single scope and letting them be destroyed automatically.

test_cases.label: Two widgets
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Base constructed
Derived(Alpha) constructed
Base constructed
Derived(Beta) constructed
Derived(Beta) destroyed
Base destroyed
Derived(Alpha) destroyed
Base destroyed

test_cases.label: Single-character names
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Base constructed
Derived(X) constructed
Base constructed
Derived(Y) constructed
Derived(Y) destroyed
Base destroyed
Derived(X) destroyed
Base destroyed

test_cases.label: Longer names
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Base constructed
Derived(Engine) constructed
Base constructed
Derived(Gearbox) constructed
Derived(Gearbox) destroyed
Base destroyed
Derived(Engine) destroyed
Base destroyed

title: Multi-level construction order
prompt_md:
## Goal

Extend the construction-order idea to a three-level hierarchy `Animal` -> `Pet` -> `Dog`. Each level prints a message in its constructor and destructor so you can verify that construction runs from the most-base class downward, and destruction runs from the most-derived class upward.

## Input

- Line 1: a string `name` (no spaces).
- Line 2: a string `breed` (no spaces).

## Output

With one `Dog` created in a scope and then destroyed:

```
Animal constructed
Pet(<name>) constructed
Dog(<name>, <breed>) constructed
Dog(<name>, <breed>) destroyed
Pet(<name>) destroyed
Animal destroyed
```

## Constraints

- `Animal` prints `Animal constructed` / `Animal destroyed`.
- `Pet` (inherits from `Animal`) stores the name and prints `Pet(<name>) constructed` / `Pet(<name>) destroyed`.
- `Dog` (inherits from `Pet`) stores the breed and prints `Dog(<name>, <breed>) constructed` / `Dog(<name>, <breed>) destroyed`.
- The output order must result from the language's construction/destruction rules, not from manually arranging print statements.

test_cases.label: Typical dog
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Animal constructed
Pet(Rex) constructed
Dog(Rex, Shepherd) constructed
Dog(Rex, Shepherd) destroyed
Pet(Rex) destroyed
Animal destroyed

test_cases.label: Short names
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Animal constructed
Pet(Bo) constructed
Dog(Bo, Pug) constructed
Dog(Bo, Pug) destroyed
Pet(Bo) destroyed
Animal destroyed

test_cases.label: Longer names
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Animal constructed
Pet(Maximilian) constructed
Dog(Maximilian, GoldenRetriever) constructed
Dog(Maximilian, GoldenRetriever) destroyed
Pet(Maximilian) destroyed
Animal destroyed
